quotes.verbatim_quote:
(aka 4th chords) are built by stacking 4th intervals above the lowest
note of the chord.
quotes.why_load_bearing: This is the foundational definition of quartal chords — the structural rule that distinguishes them from tertian harmony.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
Quartal chords sound more open and modern compared to other chord types and can
be used over any jazz standard in your comping and solos.
quotes.why_load_bearing: This gives the player a concrete application rule — quartal chords are not context-specific but universally applicable over jazz standards in both comping and soloing.

quotes.verbatim_quote: Quartal Chords Based on the C Dorian Scale (String Set 2-3-4-5)
quotes.why_load_bearing: Identifies the specific string set for the first chord-shape exercise, anchoring the voicings to a particular region of the fretboard.

quotes.verbatim_quote: Quartal Chords Based on the C Dorian Scale (String Set 1-2-3-4)
quotes.why_load_bearing: Identifies the second string set for quartal voicings, showing the player how to transpose the same harmonic idea to higher strings.
